source.title: PDD vs Alibaba vs JD.com Investment Pattern Comparison

extractions.claim: PDD spends far less on R&D ($1.8B) than Alibaba ($8.1B) and JD.com ($3.2B), but is growing R&D fastest at 41% YoY
extractions.evidence: Annual R&D comparison (latest available): Alibaba ~$7.9-8.1B (FY ending March 2025, +9% YoY), JD.com ~$3.2B (FY2025, +30.5% YoY), PDD ~$1.8B (TTM ending Q3 2025, R&D growing 23-41% YoY by quarter). JD.com Q4 2025 R&D surged 52% YoY to RMB 6.7B.

verification.verifier_notes: Alibaba from MacroTrends/earnings releases. JD.com from Q4/FY2025 earnings release. PDD from quarterly earnings releases.

extractions.claim: Alibaba plans $53B AI/cloud capex over 3 years — the biggest private Chinese entity allocation. PDD has zero comparable hard-asset capex plan.
extractions.evidence: Alibaba announced plans to invest at least RMB 380 billion (US$53 billion) over three years to advance cloud computing and AI infrastructure. Capital expenditure surged 80% YoY to RMB 31.9B ($4.5B) in the most recent quarter. PDD has no comparable capex announcement; its asset-light model directs investment through opex.

verification.verifier_notes: Alibaba capex from earnings call and multiple news reports. PDD's lack of capex program confirmed by absence in investor communications.

extractions.claim: JD.com's capital-intensive logistics model (40+ intelligent logistics parks, autonomous delivery) contrasts with PDD/Temu's 3PL partnership approach
extractions.evidence: JD Logistics operates more than 40 intelligent logistics parks with AI-powered scheduling robots and autonomous delivery vehicles. Fulfillment expenses at 6.5% of revenues. PDD/Temu uses third-party logistics (3PL) partnerships and only 13 self-owned warehouses globally.

verification.verifier_notes: JD logistics data from JD.com investor materials. PDD/Temu warehouse count from industry reporting.

extractions.claim: PDD's investment thesis is opex-driven growth vs peers' capex-driven growth: merchant subsidies (RMB 100B/3yr), R&D acceleration, and geographic ad reallocation rather than hard infrastructure
extractions.evidence: PDD investment channels: (1) RMB 100B merchant/farmer support program over 3 years, (2) R&D growing 41% YoY, (3) 13 Temu warehouses (modest vs JD's 40+ logistics parks), (4) S&M reallocation from US to Europe. Alibaba: $53B AI/cloud capex. JD: logistics park buildout + R&D surge. PDD's approach is platform economics — invest in ecosystem participants rather than owned infrastructure.

verification.verifier_notes: Synthesis of data from all three companies' financial disclosures. The characterization of PDD as 'opex-driven growth' is analyst interpretation.
